name: taskwright
description: Agentic task board for VS Code — triage work and dispatch isolated Claude Code or Codex sessions per task, on a git-native Backlog.md backbone.

taskwright.taskIdDisplay.description: Controls how task IDs are shown in task cards and list rows: full (TASK-123), number (123), or hidden.

taskwright.claimIdentity.markdownDescription: Identity that advisory task claims are attributed to (e.g. `@alice`). When empty, your OS username is used, prefixed with `@`.

taskwright.claimStalenessHours.markdownDescription: Hours after which another session's advisory claim is treated as stale (abandoned): stale claims are shown as such and can be re-claimed without an override prompt. Set to `0` to disable staleness (every live claim by another identity then requires confirmation to override).

enumDescriptions: The board is local, git-ignored working files — no versioning.
The board is additionally snapshotted onto a dedicated `taskwright-board` ref you can explicitly push/pull to share it — no live/background sync.
The board lives in a hidden worktree (`.taskwright/board`) on the `taskwright-board` branch with automatic event-driven commit/sync — no interval polling.
taskwright.sync.mode.markdownDescription: How the Taskwright board is versioned. `off` (default): the board is local, git-ignored working files — no versioning. `git`: the board is additionally snapshotted onto a dedicated `taskwright-board` ref you can explicitly push/pull to share it — no live/background sync. `git-auto`: the board's one physical home is a hidden worktree at `.taskwright/board` (branch `taskwright-board`) with event-driven auto-commit/auto-sync. Switch modes via the **Taskwright: Enable Board Sync** command, which performs the required migration — do not flip this setting by hand.

taskwright.sync.ref.markdownDescription: Name of the dedicated orphan branch that stores the versioned board (kept off your code branches). Change only if it collides with an existing branch.

taskwright.sync.remote.markdownDescription: Git remote used to push/pull the board ref when `taskwright.sync.mode` is `git`. Uses your existing push credentials — no separate account or service.

taskwright.sync.installHooks.markdownDescription: Opt-in: install Windows-safe `pre-push`/`post-merge` git hooks that automatically push/pull the board ref alongside your normal code push/pull. Off by default — the explicit Push/Pull Board commands always work without this.

taskwright.setupWritesProjectMcpJson.markdownDescription: Opt-in: when running "Set Up Claude Code Integration", also write a project-local `.mcp.json` and copy `scripts/taskwright-mcp.cjs` into the repo, so a Claude Code session opened in this repo gets the Taskwright MCP without the user-scope CLI registration. Off by default. The launcher resolves the primary Taskwright checkout's built server, so this is intended for Taskwright checkouts and their worktrees.

enumDescriptions: Claude Code — dispatch prompts address a Claude Code session and point at the /execute-task skill.
Codex — dispatch prompts address a Codex session and point at the /execute-task skill Taskwright installs as a native .agents/skills package.
taskwright.dispatchAgent.markdownDescription: Which coding agent a dispatched task targets. Selects the built-in dispatch profile: prompt template phrasing, the `/execute-task` entry-point wording, and the suggested interactive terminal command. A custom `#taskwright.dispatchTemplate#` always wins untouched. The headless-mode guardrail (`claude -p`/`--print`, `codex exec`, `--headless`/`--non-interactive`) applies regardless of the selected agent — subscription safety is a principle, not a per-agent feature.

taskwright.dispatchTemplate.markdownDescription: Template for the dispatch prompt copied to the clipboard. Supports `{{id}}`, `{{title}}`, `{{status}}`, `{{priority}}`, `{{labels}}`, `{{description}}`, `{{acceptanceCriteria}}`, `{{plan}}`, `{{worktree}}`, and `{{filePath}}`. Leave empty to use the built-in template for the selected `#taskwright.dispatchAgent#`.

taskwright.dispatchCreateWorktree.markdownDescription: Dispatch each task into its own isolated git worktree at `.worktrees/<branch>` (the default), so parallel sessions never share a working directory or active task. Set to `false` to dispatch into the workspace root instead. Falls back to the workspace root automatically when the folder is not a git repository. Note: the `false` opt-out is incompatible with the `/execute-task` auto-close flow (which requires a worktree); such a dispatch pastes an in-place manual-TDD prompt instead.

taskwright.dispatchOpenTerminal.markdownDescription: When dispatching a task creates a worktree, open an integrated terminal in that worktree directory.

taskwright.dispatchTerminalCommand.markdownDescription: When a dispatch opens a worktree terminal (`taskwright.dispatchOpenTerminal`), run this command in it. Supports the dispatch placeholders, notably `{{handoffFile}}` (path to the saved prompt) plus `{{id}}`, `{{title}}`, `{{worktree}}`. Leave empty to just open the terminal. To auto-start a session, seed an **interactive** session of your `#taskwright.dispatchAgent#` from the handoff file — e.g. bash: `claude "$(cat {{handoffFile}})"` or `codex "$(cat {{handoffFile}})"`, PowerShell: `claude (Get-Content -Raw '{{handoffFile}}')`. Headless/non-interactive commands (`claude -p`/`--print`, `codex exec`, `--headless`/`--non-interactive`) are metered and are skipped with a warning.

taskwright.intakeTemplate.markdownDescription: Template for the "Categorize with Claude" intake prompt. Supports `{{dump}}` (your raw notes), `{{labels}}`, `{{statuses}}`, and `{{priorities}}` (the board's vocabulary). Leave empty to use the built-in template.

taskwright.enforceWorktreeIsolation.markdownDescription: Install a managed `pre-commit` git hook that blocks committing a dispatched task branch from the primary working tree — the failure mode where a parallel agent escapes its `.worktrees/<branch>` and clobbers a sibling branch. Bypass a single commit with `git commit --no-verify`. Set to `false` to remove the hook. When enabled, adds a managed fenced block to the project's `.husky/pre-commit` (or `.git/hooks/pre-commit` when husky is absent) and copies a small guard script into the gitignored `.taskwright/hooks/`.

taskwright.mergeMode.markdownDescription: How `request_merge` integrates a finished task once it reaches the head of the merge queue. `manual-review` (default) parks it in **Pending Review** and waits for you to Approve on the board before fast-forward-merging to `main`. `auto-merge` (**Awaiting Merge**) fast-forward-merges with no review. `auto-pr` (**Awaiting PR**) pushes the branch and opens a GitHub pull request instead. The mode also names the intermediate board column.

taskwright.mergeVerifyCommands.markdownDescription: Commands `request_merge` runs (in order, in the worktree) to verify a task before and after it reaches the head of the merge queue. Any non-zero exit aborts the merge and returns the failing output to the agent. Set to `[]` to skip verification.

taskwright.mergeQueueStaleMinutes.markdownDescription: Minutes after which the active head of the merge queue (a task mid-merge) is treated as abandoned — a crashed agent — and reclaimed so the next task can proceed. Set to `0` to reclaim immediately (not recommended).

taskwright.mergeVerifyTimeoutMinutes.markdownDescription: Minutes each `taskwright.mergeVerifyCommands` command may run before `request_merge` kills it and aborts with code `verify_timeout` (distinct from `verify_failed`, which means the command actually went red). Raise this for repos with long test suites so verification can actually finish. An agent can also raise it per call via the `verifyTimeoutMinutes` parameter of the `request_merge` MCP tool (bounded by `taskwright.mergeVerifyTimeoutMaxMinutes`). Note that concurrent `request_merge` calls no longer run their verify suites at the same time — they take turns via a shared verify slot — so a slow verify is rarely a sign that this value is too low.

taskwright.mergeVerifyTimeoutMaxMinutes.markdownDescription: Upper bound (minutes) for the per-call `verifyTimeoutMinutes` parameter of the `request_merge` MCP tool — an agent-requested timeout above this is clamped down to it. Set to `0` (default) for no cap.

commands.title: Taskwright: Open Board in Editor Tab
Taskwright: Enable Board Sync (move board off code branches)
Taskwright: Force Claim Task (override dependency gate)
Taskwright: Set Up Claude Code Integration (MCP + CLAUDE.md)
Taskwright: Set Up Codex Integration (MCP + skills)

scripts.build: bun run compile:webview && bun run build:css && bun run compile
scripts.test:e2e: bun run build && tsc -p tsconfig.e2e.json && bun scripts/run-e2e.ts
scripts.proof: bun run build && playwright test dispatch.spec board-indicators.spec
scripts.ci: bun run check:engines && bun run lint && bun run typecheck && bun run depcheck && bun run audit:gate && bun run licenses:check && bun run test && bun run build && bun run test:playwright && bun run test:cdp && bun run test:e2e
